Real-ear verification improves fitting precision

Probe microphone verification checks hearing aid output in the actual ear and helps clinicians identify under- or over-amplification compared with prescriptive targets. Use this when explaining why verification is not optional in a quality-focused fitting.
asha_ha,apso_s21,rem_review
Follow-up after hearing aid fitting is part of care

Adult hearing aid follow-up should review concerns, insertion, maintenance, comfort, sound quality, telephone use, assistive technology, and data logging when available. Use this to justify structured follow-up instead of a one-time device delivery model.
nice_ng98,asha_ha
Hearing aids and cognition require careful wording

Hearing loss is associated with cognitive outcomes, but clinicians should avoid promising dementia prevention. The ACHIEVE trial supports nuanced counseling, especially for older adults at increased risk.
achieve,nice_ng98,lancet_dementia
BPPV care should be efficient and evidence-guided
The BPPV guideline emphasizes accurate positional diagnosis, appropriate repositioning maneuvers, and reducing unnecessary imaging and vestibular suppressants in typical cases.
aao_bppv,bppv_pubmed
Tinnitus care should focus on education, distress, sleep, and referral safety
Guidelines support education, assessment of impact, hearing care when relevant, sound support, psychological support pathways, and red-flag referral.
nice_tinnitus,aao_tinnitus,ncrar_ptm
Pediatric hearing loss requires timely diagnosis and intervention
JCIH emphasizes early hearing detection and intervention to support language, literacy, communication, and psychosocial outcomes.
jcih_2019,aaa_ped_assess
Pediatric amplification needs child-specific verification
Pediatric fittings require ear-specific measures, verification, repeated monitoring, family support, and adjustment as the child grows.
aaa_ped_amp,jcih_2019
Remote microphone technology supports access in noise and distance

Remote microphones can improve the signal reaching the listener when noise, distance, or reverberation reduce speech access. Use with classroom, workplace, CAPD, and complex speech-in-noise counseling.
remote_mic,asha_capd
CAPD requires differential diagnosis and interdisciplinary thinking
CAPD guidelines emphasize careful case history, appropriate testing, differential diagnosis, and intervention planning that includes environmental and compensatory supports.
asha_capd,aaa_capd
CI referral should not wait until all hearing aid benefit is exhausted beyond function
CI referral guidance supports earlier consideration when functional benefit from hearing aids is limited. Counseling should focus on speech access, quality of life, and patient goals.
nice_ci,acia_adult_ci,acia_ped_ci
Noise-induced hearing loss is preventable
Public health guidance emphasizes lowering level, reducing duration, increasing distance, taking breaks, and using hearing protection when noise cannot be avoided.
cdc_nihl,niosh_noise
Genetic hearing loss is common in prelingual hearing loss
GeneReviews states that genetic causes account for a large proportion of prelingual hearing loss, which supports genetics referral counseling when appropriate.
gene_overview,gene_gr
Congenital CMV hearing loss can be delayed or progressive
CDC and professional position statements support monitoring because cCMV-related hearing loss can be present at birth or appear later and may change over time.
cdc_cmv,aaa_cmv,cmv_aao
